sections.content:
I see that frame 2 and frame 3 share some blocks. Looking at the data:
- Frame 2 blocks include (35,23): b, (35,26): w, (35,29): b, (35,32): S, (35,35): p, (35,38): b, (35,41): g, (35,44): S
- Frame 3 blocks include (35,23): b, (35,26): w, (35,29): b, (35,32): S, (35,35): p, (35,38): b, (35,41): g, (35,44): S
These are the same blocks! So frames 2 and 3 share the row at 35. This means the frames overlap.
Let me reconsider the structure. Looking at the image, I see 3 frames stacked vertically. But the blocks at row 35 appear in both frame 2 and frame 3 lists. This suggests the frames are:
- Frame 1: rows 17-23 (top row at 17, left/right cols at 20, 23)
- Frame 2: rows 26-32 (top row at 26, left/right cols at 29, 32)
- Frame 3: rows 35-44 (top row at 35, left/right cols at 38, 41, bottom row at 44)
Wait, but frame 3 has a bottom row at 44, while frames 1 and 2 don't seem to have bottom rows. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking more carefully at frame 1:
- Top row: (17,23), (17,26), (17,29), (17,32), (17,35) - 5 blocks
- Left col: (20,23), (23,23) - 2 blocks
- Right col: (20,35), (23,35) - 2 blocks
Total: 9 blocks
Frame 2:
- Top row: (26,23), (26,26), (26,29), (26,32), (26,35), (26,38), (26,41), (26,44) - 8 blocks
- Left col: (29,23), (32,23) - 2 blocks
- Right col: (29,35), (32,35) - 2 blocks
Total: 12 blocks
Frame 3:
- Top row: (35,23), (35,26), (35,29), (35,32), (35,35), (35,38), (35,41), (35,44) - 8 blocks
- Left col: (38,23), (41,23) - 2 blocks
- Right col: (38,35), (41,35) - 2 blocks
- Bottom row: (44,23), (44,26), (44,29), (44,32), (44,35) - 5 blocks
Total: 17 blocks
Wait, but frames 2 and 3 share the row at 35. So frame 2's bottom row IS frame 3's top row. This means the frames are connected/overlapping.
Hmm, actually I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me look at the image again. The image shows 3 distinct frames, each with:
- Red button on left
- Green button on right
- A rectangular ring of colored blocks
- Yellow markers in the center
Let me just try clicking the red button for frame 1 and see what happens. The red button for frame 1 is at (16,19)-(19,21), so I'll click at (17, 20).
